old: He issued an important speech at the meeting.
new: He delivered an important speech at the meeting.
reason: You deliver or give a speech, not 'issue' one; 'issued a speech' is unnatural English.

old: He quickly came to his senses and reacted.
new: He quickly snapped back to his senses.
reason: 他很快反应过来了 means he quickly came around/reacted; 'came to his senses and reacted' is redundant.
Row 3397 (防 'guard against') already uses 要防小偷 as sentence_1; using a nearly identical sentence for this row (防 'to prevent') is a near-duplicate across rows of the same character.

old: You need to guard against thieves.
reason: Updated to match corrected sentence_1; also the old translation reflects 'guard against' (row 3397's definition) rather than 'prevent'.

old: She paid a great deal of time and energy.
new: She devoted a great deal of time and energy.
reason: In English you 'devote' or 'expend' time and energy, not 'pay' it; 'paid time' is unnatural.
'Did up a plan' is unnatural English; 'put together a plan' is the natural phrasing.
